finding: Klebsiella, E. coli, Pseudomonas, anaerobes, β-hemolytic streptococcus, mixed

differentials.name: Aspiration pneumonia = gravitational pneumonia: mixed gram-negative organisms, anaerobes (b)  granulomatous infection
differentials.raw: Aspiration pneumonia = gravitational pneumonia: mixed gram-negative organisms, anaerobes (b)  granulomatous infection

differentials.name: TB: cavitation indicates active infectious disease with risk for hematogenous / bronchogenic dissemination
differentials.raw: TB: cavitation indicates active infectious disease with risk for hematogenous / bronchogenic dissemination

differentials.name: Fungus: coccidioidomycosis, nocardiosis (in immunocompromised), histoplasmosis, blastomycosis, mucormycosis, sporotrichosis, aspergillosis, cryptococcosis
differentials.raw: Fungus: coccidioidomycosis, nocardiosis (in immunocompromised), histoplasmosis, blastomycosis, mucormycosis, sporotrichosis, aspergillosis, cryptococcosis

differentials.name: Angioinvasion → septic lung infarction followed by cavity formation:
differentials.raw: Angioinvasion → septic lung infarction followed by cavity formation:

differentials.name: NEOPLASM
differentials.raw: NEOPLASM (a)  primary lung tumor: 16% of peripheral lung cancers (in particular squamous cell carcinoma (30%); also in bronchioloalveolar cell carcinoma (b)  metastasis (usually multiple)

differentials.name: Squamous cell carcinoma (in ⅔): nasopharynx, esophagus, cervix
differentials.raw: Squamous cell carcinoma (in ⅔): nasopharynx, esophagus, cervix

differentials.name: Lymphoma (rare): with adenopathy; cavities often ← opportunistic infection with nocardiosis + cryptococcosis
differentials.raw: Lymphoma (rare): with adenopathy; cavities often ← opportunistic infection with nocardiosis + cryptococcosis

context: gram-negative organisms √  very thin-walled cavities less likely to follow the apical distribution of TB / histoplasmosis Aspergillus, Mucorales, Candida, torulosis, P. aeruginosa (c)  parasitic infestation: hydatid disease
